summary: The unit tests in matrix-generator.test.ts cover the core generator well across the main axes (cartesian product, include/exclude, fail-fast, max-parallel, max_size validation) and include a few meaningful edge cases. The workflow.test.ts adds useful structural checks and actionlint validation. However, several gaps weaken the suite: (1) index.ts (the CLI entry point) has zero test coverage — no tests for missing file, invalid JSON, or the MATRIX_JSON_START/END markers; (2) run-act-tests.ts is a standalone imperative script rather than a bun:test file, so it is silently excluded from `bun test` runs; (3) important edge cases are missing — overlapping include entries (should not add a new job), all-excludes-applied (zero remaining combos), config with only feature flags and no OS/language axes, and negative or zero max_parallel; (4) the include-overlap counting logic is exercised only indirectly, with no dedicated test asserting that an include matching an existing combo is counted correctly; (5) workflow structure tests rely on substring matching which is brittle (e.g. 'bun' anywhere satisfies the 'installs Bun' check). Overall the test suite provides reasonable confidence for the happy path of the generator but would miss regressions in the CLI layer and several boundary conditions.
